sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Sunderland's last 5 matches show poor form with only 1 win and 4 losses, conceding multiple goals in most games. West Ham United, on the other hand, have a stronger recent record with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, scoring consistently. This suggests West Ham are in better shape coming into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 head-to-head matches, West Ham have dominated with 1 win and 1 draw, remaining unbeaten. Historically, West Ham have won 3 of the last 5 encounters against Sunderland, indicating a psychological edge for the Hammers.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: West Ham’s Jarrod Bowen is in excellent form, with 4 goals and 3 assists in the last 5 matches, posing a significant threat. Sunderland’s Daniel Ballard has been solid defensively but lacks the attacking output to counter West Ham’s firepower.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Streaks
sections.text: West Ham’s streak of scoring in recent games and tendency for over 2.5 goals in matches aligns with their attacking form. Sunderland’s defense has struggled, failing to keep a clean sheet in 7 straight head-to-head games. Both teams also tend to see fewer than 10.5 corners and 4.5 cards, suggesting a controlled game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains goalless between Sunderland and West Ham United at the Stadium of Light. Both teams have shown moments of promise but have failed to capitalize on their opportunities. West Ham have dominated possession and shown more intent in the final third, while Sunderland have had their share of chances, including a notable big chance missed. Neither side has been able to break the deadlock, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Sunderland
halftimeSections.text: Sunderland have struggled to maintain control of the game, with significantly less possession compared to their opponents. Despite this, they’ve created a big chance and managed a couple of shots on target. Their entries into the penalty area are on par with West Ham, showing they can penetrate when given the opportunity. Defensively, they’ve held firm, with key clearances and a solid structure preventing West Ham from converting their dominance into goals. However, their lower success rate in duels, especially in the air, could be a concern moving forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: West Ham United
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United have been the more dominant side in the first half, controlling the ball for the majority of the time and pushing forward with frequent entries into the final third. They’ve won more duels, particularly in aerial challenges, showcasing their physical edge. Although they’ve had shots on target and a higher expected goals tally, they’ve lacked the cutting edge to create a big chance. Their ability to win possession in the final third suggests they’re close to unlocking Sunderland’s defense, but they’ll need to be more clinical in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw West Ham start with strong attacking intent, consistently pushing forward in the opening minutes. Sunderland responded with a brief spell of pressure around the midway point of the half, showing intent to counter. West Ham regained momentum with a sustained period of dominance, especially towards the latter stages of the half, mounting wave after wave of attacks. However, Sunderland finished the half on a high note with a flurry of attacking moves, hinting at a potential shift as they head into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Sunderland, Habib Diarra has been a standout in midfield with a notable presence in attack, though he missed a big chance. Daniel Ballard has been a rock at the back with crucial clearances and also posed a threat upfront with a shot on target. On West Ham’s side, Niclas Füllkrug and Jarrod Bowen have been active in attack and could be the ones to break the deadlock with their positioning and threat in front of goal. El Hadji Malick Diouf has also shown promise with key passes and a shot on target, potentially a difference-maker in the second half.
